chapter.title: The deed of a pald MAN

paragraphs.text: DRESSED IN SACK-CLOTH Lavra <of the Great Oasis?> were Abba John, formerly lector at the i Great Church in Constantinople, Abba Eustathios the Roman, and When I and my companion were in Alexandria, one day we went to Abba Theodore, all three of whom were sick. When they had been the Church of <Saint> Theodosios. A bald man accosted us who H captured, Abba John said to the barbarians: "Take me to the city was wearing sack-cloth down to his knees. He seemed to be insanc. i and I will have the bishop give you twenty-four pieces of gold’. So Abba Sophronios said to me: “Give me a coin and you shall see the i one of the barbarians led him off and brought him near to the city. virtue of this man who is approaching us’. 1 gave him five coppers i Abba John went in to the bishop. Abba Leo was in the city at that <pholeis> which he took and gave to the onc who seemed to be time and so were some others of the fathers; that is why they were insane, who received them without a word. Keeping ourselves out H aot captured. Abba John went in and began to implore the bishop of sight, we followed him. When he liad turned the street-corner, he H to give the bacbarian the twenty-four pieces of gold, but the bishop stretched out his right hand (in which he held the coins) towards i could only find eight. He was willing to give these to the barbarian, heaven, held it up high, and then prostrated himself before God- ] but he would nol take them. ‘Either give me twenty-four pieces of and went his way, leaving the coins on the ground. | gold or the monk’, he said.
